description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a crustacean, specifically resembling a crayfish or lobster, with a segmented body and a hard, dark brown exoskeleton. The front features two large, robust claws (chelae) that are prominently extended, and several walking legs are visible along the sides of the thorax. The cephalothorax is broad and tapers into a segmented abdomen, ending in a fan-like tail. Two long, thin antennae extend from the head, and the eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front. The surface appears hard and slightly glossy, with subtle textural details that mimic the natural roughness of a crustacean shell. The overall coloration is a mix of dark brown, black, and hints of green, giving it a lifelike, aquatic appearance.
description: A lifelike, dark brown crustacean with large claws, segmented body, and long antennae, featuring a hard, slightly glossy shell.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, both large claws, and extended antennae.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the broad cephalothorax, segmentation, and symmetrical arrangement of legs and claws.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile, with claws and walking legs clearly visible.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the two large claws, head, and the forward-facing antennae.
